ops.title: Installed Claude Code hook commands must fail open at the DISPATCH layer (uvx/CLI routing), not just inside the handler — a published wheel predating the verb exits 2 and blocks every prompt machine-wide
ops.body:
The cc-steer `live install-hook` incident (2026-07-13): the hook handler itself was
carefully fail-open, but the installed command `uvx cc-steer live hook` exited 2 on
the published 0.10.0 wheel (click UsageError for the not-yet-released `live` group),
and exit 2 on `UserPromptSubmit` **blocks every prompt on the machine**. Caught by
testing the exact installed command string (`echo '{}' | uvx cc-steer live hook; echo $?`)
immediately after install, before any real session hit it.

**Why:** fail-open contracts are usually audited inside the handler; the wrapper
layers (uvx resolution, stale uvx caches serving old versions, CLI command routing,
network failure) sit OUTSIDE that guard and produce the exact exit code that blocks.

**How to apply:** when installing any Claude Code hook that shells through a
published CLI: (1) the written command template must exit 0 + emit nothing when the
underlying verb is missing or resolution fails; (2) version-pin the resolution
(`--from 'pkg>=X'`) so stale tool caches can't serve a wheel predating the verb;
(3) always smoke the exact installed command string with the target exit-code
semantics in mind before leaving it wired; (4) release ordering: the verb must exist
on PyPI before install-hook is usable outside the dev checkout. See
[[cc-notes-cli-drift-breaks-journaling]] for the sibling pattern (CLI drift breaking
wrappers).
